title: Coverage Gate checks per-shard instead of merged — always fails when sharding
description:
The 'Coverage Gate' step in Equoria Quality Gate (.github/workflows/test.yml or similar) checks each test shard's coverage against a 70% threshold independently, which is architecturally wrong when using sharded Jest.

Each shard runs a subset of tests against the ENTIRE source tree, producing a coverage report that covers all source files but only exercises the subset the shard's tests hit. Per-shard coverage therefore naturally reports ~20-30%, not because coverage is low but because that shard only ran a third of the test suite.

Observed on run 24731832109 (master @ ecc7eeec):
- Shard 1 — lines: 22.72% branches: 21.69%
- Shard 2 — lines: 25.03% branches: 20.2%
- Shard 3 — lines: 26.24% branches: 23.7%
- Gate fails with 'Coverage gate failed — minimum threshold is 70%'

The fix: after the 3 shards upload their coverage-shard-N artifacts, the Coverage Gate job must MERGE the summaries (e.g., using istanbul-merge, nyc merge, or a node script that unions the coverage objects) into a single total, then apply the 70% threshold to that merged result.

Current (broken) loop:
  for shard in 1 2 3; do
    SUMMARY=./coverage-shard-${shard}/coverage-summary.json
    LINES=$(node -e "const s=require('$SUMMARY').total; console.log(s.lines.pct)")
    if (( $(echo "$LINES < $THRESHOLD" | bc -l) )); then FAIL=1; fi
  done

Should be: merge 3 coverage-shard-N/coverage-final.json (or lcov) → compute total.lines.pct on the merged total → apply threshold once.

Acceptance criteria:
- Coverage Gate uses the merged total, not per-shard values
- On master with full test suite running, Coverage Gate passes if the union coverage is ≥ 70%
- No test.skip, no threshold lowering to hide the issue
- 21R doctrine: no false green, no disabling the gate

title: Story 3-2: Horse Detail View
Cross-suite contamination breaks health-monitoring + parameter-pollution tests
description:
Two integration test suites pass in isolation but fail when run as part of the full backend test suite (npm test -- --runInBand --retryTimes=1).

Failing tests (evidence from 2026-04-21 pre-push run):
- tests/integration/health-monitoring-integration.test.mjs:185 — register endpoint returns 500 instead of 201
- __tests__/integration/security/parameter-pollution.test.mjs:50 — Prisma FK constraint violated on horses_userId_fkey

Isolation verified:
- health-monitoring-integration: 17/17 pass in 7.9s
- parameter-pollution: 42/42 pass in 5.3s

Full-suite run (663s, 276 suites, runInBand, retry=1): 3 tests failing across these 2 suites.

Likely causes (to investigate):
- Memory pressure — observed 565MB threshold exceeded warnings late in the run
- Shared email patterns: health-monitoring uses hardcoded 'healthintegration@test.com'; parameter-pollution uses 'test-${Date.now()}@example.com' and its afterAll deletes ALL users matching 'test-' which could also break other suites
- FK contamination: some earlier suite may leave dangling refs or delete users mid-run
- Prisma connection pool exhaustion at the 10+ minute mark

Bisect strategy:
- Identify which earlier suite(s) break these two specifically
- Remove/fix the contaminator rather than quarantining the symptom

This is Epic 9A-1 tech debt — 'Stabilize flaky tests + restore pre-push hook' — that still needs completion.
notes:
## Investigation findings (2026-04-21)

### Bisect results
- health-monitoring-integration alone: 17/17 pass (7.9s)
- parameter-pollution alone: 42/42 pass (5.3s)
- __tests__/integration/security/ (8 suites, 201 tests): all pass (26.6s)
- __tests__/integration/ (28 suites, 489 tests): all pass (100s)
- tests/integration/ (35 suites, 433 tests): all pass (102s)
- __tests__/integration + tests/integration combined (63 suites, 922 tests): all pass (198s)
- __tests__/ + modules/ (106 suites, 2431 tests): all pass (170s) — INCLUDES parameter-pollution
- __tests__/ + tests/ together: OOM / V8 heap crash

### Conclusion
- Contaminator is NOT in integration directories
- Contaminator is somewhere in `tests/` (non-integration), `seed/`, or `models/`
- Under the full 276-suite load, memory pressure + Prisma connection-pool disruption breaks the later-running parameter-pollution and health-monitoring suites

### Likely root cause
- 28 test files still call `prisma.$disconnect()` mid-run (grep for `^\s*(await\s+)?prisma\.\$disconnect\(\)`)
- Known pattern: most of `tests/integration/*.test.mjs` already has comments "prisma.$disconnect() removed — global teardown handles disconnection" — the cleanup was partially done, never finished
- Remaining offenders concentrate in `tests/` (non-integration), `__tests__/integration/` token-rotation / auth-cookies / rate-limiting / session-lifecycle / advance-onboarding / register-starter-horse, and several `tests/services/`, `tests/unit/`

### Fix strategy
Remove `prisma.$disconnect()` from the 28 offenders. Global teardown (backend/tests/teardown.mjs) is the only place that should disconnect. Needs per-file verification — some may rely on the disconnect as a side effect (unlikely but possible).

### Workaround (not a fix)
- Tests pass in isolation and in large groups
- `--no-verify` push is defensible for YAML-only changes that can't affect backend tests
- CI will run its own fresh suite against a clean DB on GitHub Actions

title: Remove X-Test-Skip-Csrf bypass across all backend tests (21R: no bypass evidence)
description:
The app mounts 'doubleCsrfProtection' from csrf-csrf on authRouter (backend/app.mjs:151) and adminRouter (:157). That middleware does not honor any 'x-test-skip-csrf' header. A different, custom middleware 'applyCsrfProtection' in backend/middleware/csrf.mjs:176 does honor it — but it is not the middleware actually mounted in the app.

Dozens of existing tests set 'X-Test-Skip-Csrf: true' to bypass CSRF validation. In the current setup, that header does nothing, so every mutation test (POST/PUT/DELETE/PATCH on authRouter routes) fails with 403 'Invalid CSRF token' before hitting any real controller logic. Evidence: __tests__/integration/security/parameter-pollution.test.mjs line 111 'should reject array-syntax parameters' returns 403 when it should return 400.

21R doctrine explicitly forbids bypass-header evidence: 'Do not cite tests that use x-test-skip-csrf, x-test-bypass-auth, x-test-bypass-rate-limit, x-test-user, x-test-bypass-ownership, VITE_E2E_TEST, or route interception as beta readiness evidence.'

CORRECT FIX (no shortcuts):
Refactor every test that uses X-Test-Skip-Csrf to fetch and submit a real CSRF token the way production clients do:
1. Introduce a shared helper 'getCsrfToken(agent, cookies)' in backend/tests/helpers/ that GETs /auth/csrf-token, returns the token + the _csrf cookie so subsequent mutations can send both.
2. For each affected test file: at setup time, create user, get auth cookie/token, call the helper to get CSRF token + cookie. Remove X-Test-Skip-Csrf header. On every mutation, set both the auth cookie, the _csrf cookie, and X-CSRF-Token header.
3. Delete the unused applyCsrfProtection function from middleware/csrf.mjs so it can't be re-mounted later.

FORBIDDEN SHORTCUTS (do not do):
- Do not change app.mjs to mount applyCsrfProtection in test env. That ships a bypass pathway gated on JEST_WORKER_ID — tests would then exercise a path production traffic never touches.
- Do not add a global jest beforeAll hook that sets a dummy CSRF secret or similar bypass.
- Do not --no-verify push to move tests past the hook.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- grep -r 'x-test-skip-csrf\|X-Test-Skip-Csrf' backend returns zero matches outside of deleted code / code comments describing why it was removed.
- All CSRF-enforcing integration tests pass against the real doubleCsrfProtection middleware.
- app.mjs continues to mount csrfProtection (not applyCsrfProtection) on authRouter + adminRouter.
- applyCsrfProtection dead-code is removed from middleware/csrf.mjs, and its tests are removed too.
- Pre-push hook runs clean on master (no cascade of 403s).

description:
Salvage the small, ship-ready, well-scoped security hardening from the abandoned 'feat/21r-auth-4-csrf-collapse' WIP (which was misnamed — see closed Equoria-mm9k for context).

## Reference

Preservation: `origin/wip-preserve/2026-04-27-abandoned-feat-21r-auth-4-csrf-collapse` (commit `be910c45`). The full WIP is archived there. The commit message lists which files are SALVAGE (this issue) vs DISCARD (Equoria-ighs).

## Scope (extract these files from preservation; ship as one focused PR)

- NEW backend/utils/runtimeSecretPolicy.mjs — exports DEPLOYABLE_ENVS, isDeployableEnvironment(nodeEnv), getSecretValidationError(name, value, env). Detects placeholder strings + committed test-only secret patterns.
- NEW backend/__tests__/unit/security/runtime-secret-policy.test.mjs — unit tests for the policy module.

title: Story 1: Authentication Middleware Infrastructure
Fix parallel isolation in advancedBreedingGeneticsAPI test suite
Refresh-token hashing at rest (CWE-352-adjacent) — proper schema migration + service rewrite
description:
Re-scope the refresh-token-hash workstream from the abandoned 'feat/21r-auth-4-csrf-collapse' WIP. The WIP attempted to switch tokenRotationService.mjs and sessionManagement.mjs to operate on a 'tokenHash' column via raw SQL, but the schema was never updated (still has 'token' column with only the docstring changed). This caused 91 test failures in the auth/session/cookie test suites.

## Why we need this

Storing raw refresh tokens at rest is a security gap: a database leak (or an attacker with read-only DB access) could replay any non-expired token. SHA-256 hashing the stored value mitigates this — the cookie still carries the raw token, but the DB only ever sees the hash.

## Reference

Preservation: `origin/wip-preserve/2026-04-27-abandoned-feat-21r-auth-4-csrf-collapse` (commit `be910c45`). The failed attempt is archived there for understanding intent. **DO NOT cherry-pick the raw-SQL approach** — it is broken without the schema migration.

## Correct scope (do this work properly, in order)

1. Write ADR for refresh-token hash storage (rationale, threat model, migration strategy).
2. New Prisma migration:
   - ADD COLUMN tokenHash VARCHAR(64) (SHA-256 hex output)
   - Backfill: UPDATE refresh_tokens SET tokenHash = encode(sha256(token::bytea), 'hex') WHERE tokenHash IS NULL
   - Verify backfill completeness
   - ALTER COLUMN tokenHash SET NOT NULL
   - CREATE UNIQUE INDEX on tokenHash
   - DROP COLUMN token (after one deploy cycle to allow rollback)
3. Update Prisma schema model: rename field 'token' to 'tokenHash' OR keep 'token' as @ignore and add 'tokenHash' field.
4. Rewrite tokenRotationService.mjs to:
   - Hash incoming raw token via SHA-256 hex
   - Use Prisma model methods (NOT raw SQL) to read/write by tokenHash
   - Never log the raw token
5. Rewrite sessionManagement.mjs the same way.
6. Update test fixtures (createTestRefreshToken in __tests__/setup.mjs and __tests__/config/test-helpers.mjs) to insert via Prisma model methods, returning {raw, hash} so tests can use either.
7. Update integration tests to use the new model API instead of raw SQL.

## Acceptance criteria

- All existing tests pass without modification of their assertions (only fixture-update needed).
- New test: insert a refresh token, assert refresh_tokens.tokenHash != raw token value.
- New test: assert tokenRotationService.findRefreshToken does not log the raw token.
- ADR committed and referenced from the schema migration.

## Out of scope

- The runtimeSecretPolicy / JWT_REFRESH_SECRET hardening — see sibling Equoria-9y69.

title: master broken: applyCsrfProtection removed from backend/middleware/csrf.mjs without updating 68 importers
description:
## Problem

`applyCsrfProtection` was removed (or never exported) from `backend/middleware/csrf.mjs` on master, but 68 files across backend/ still import the symbol. Result: 86 of 282 test suites fail to even load with `SyntaxError: The requested module '../../middleware/csrf.mjs' does not provide an export named 'applyCsrfProtection'` before any test body runs.

## Evidence (discovered 2026-04-22 via pre-push hook)

Command: `npm test -- --runInBand --retryTimes=1` with `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=6144` (ran in 1127 s, completed cleanly — not an OOM).

Result:
- Test Suites: **86 failed, 196 passed, 282 total**
- Tests: 1 failed, 3713 passed, 3714 total
- Primary failure type: `SyntaxError: does not provide an export named 'applyCsrfProtection'`

Grep evidence:
- `grep -rn applyCsrfProtection backend/ | grep -v node_modules` → 68 hits (importers + references)
- `grep -rn applyCsrfProtection backend/middleware/csrf.mjs` → 0 hits (export is gone)

## Suspected cause

Epic 21R-AUTH story 4 ("Collapse to single CSRF implementation and purge dead code") was scheduled to do exactly this — delete `applyCsrfProtection` and migrate its 68 callsites. Story 4 has not formally landed yet (per change-proposal-2026-04-21.md §4 Story 4 ACs, the migration is a coordinated single-PR operation requiring ADR-005 + security reviewer signoff). Something either:
  (a) removed the export ahead of the planned story
  (b) PR #88 (merged hotfix 21R-AUTH-1) scope-crept into story 4's territory
  (c) an unrelated merge dropped the export

Commit to investigate first: `46472444 hotfix(auth): scope refresh cookie to / so /api/auth/refresh-token works (21R-AUTH-1)` (merged via PR #88). The hotfix was nominally a two-line cookie-config change; if it actually modified csrf.mjs, that is scope creep contrary to the Story 21R-AUTH-1 AC-8 out-of-scope guardrails.

## Impact

- **Master pre-push hook fails** on any branch because master itself has 86 suites that cannot load. This blocks all non-`--no-verify` pushes until fixed.
- **Epic 21R-AUTH story 4** becomes partially done-in-place without its ADR-005 authorship, security-reviewer signoff, or coordinated importer migration — violating the AC structure the change proposal laid out.
- **Beta deployment readiness gate** (21R-6) cannot honestly produce evidence while 86 test suites fail to load.
- **CI** may be green only because it ignores pre-push hooks entirely OR because CI runs a different, narrower subset — either answer is itself a defect worth documenting.

## Suggested fix path

Treat this as either:
  (A) a hotfix: restore `applyCsrfProtection` as a thin wrapper around `doubleCsrfProtection` so the existing 68 callsites keep working (preserves green tests) — THEN do the story-4 migration properly later.
  (B) accelerate Epic 21R-AUTH story 4: write ADR-005, get security reviewer signoff, migrate all 68 callsites to `doubleCsrfProtection`, delete the compat wrapper. Single coordinated PR per the story's original plan.

(A) is faster and unblocks everyone immediately; (B) is the end-state goal. User (Heirr) decides which.

## Blocks

- beads: docs/21r-auth-1-spec-retroactive push (commit 828cb25d, local branch `docs/21r-auth-1-spec-retroactive`) — retries after this issue closes.
- Any other push-bearing work on this repo whose author respects the pre-push hook.

## Does not block

CI: Coverage Gate thresholds per-shard partial coverage against full-coverage limit
description:
Equoria Quality Gate's 'Coverage Gate' job fails on every master push because it checks each shard's partial coverage against a 70% threshold, but each shard only runs ~1/3 of the test suite.

Observed on PR #82 merge (run 24731832109):
- Shard 1 — lines: 22.72% branches: 21.69%
- Shard 2 — lines: 25.03% branches: 20.2%
- Shard 3 — lines: 26.24% branches: 23.7%
Script output:
  ❌ Shard 1 lines coverage 22.72% < 70%
  ❌ Shard 2 lines coverage 25.03% < 70%
  ❌ Shard 3 lines coverage 26.24% < 70%
  Coverage gate failed — minimum threshold is 70%

Root cause: .github/workflows/quality-gate.yml 'Merge and check coverage threshold' step loops over each downloaded shard summary instead of merging them first.

Fix direction: use nyc/istanbul merge to combine coverage-final.json from each shard into a single report, then threshold the merged totals. OR lower the per-shard threshold to a realistic ~20% if keeping per-shard.

Acceptance criteria:
- Coverage Gate job passes on master when actual merged backend coverage is ≥ threshold
- Threshold logic reflects full-suite coverage, not per-shard partial coverage
- No disabling of the gate — preserve the quality signal
